seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using Pydantic AI face immense risks when deploying AI agents that process sensitive patient data, as even minor compliance lapses can lead to patient harm and regulatory fines up to $1.5M per violation. Vex provides essential guardrails by validating agent outputs against HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and other standards, ensuring PHI is de-identified and decisions are auditable. Integrating Vex as a result_validator prevents common Pydantic AI failure modes like validation rejections and streaming failures in production healthcare applications.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires protection of Protected Health Information (PHI) with strict controls on data access, transmission, and de-identification before processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex result_validators automatically detect PHI in agent outputs and block transmission, enforcing de-identification schemas before LLM processing.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, auditable, and compliant with validation requirements for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent decisions with immutable audit trails, validating structured outputs against Part 11 schemas for regulatory inspections.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and increased penalties for unsecured PHI exposure in healthcare AI systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides real-time PHI detection and automated breach alerts, ensuring compliance with 60-day notification timelines.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls over security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy for healthcare SaaS platforms.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitoring captures all agent interactions for SOC 2 evidence, validating against security and privacy trust criteria.

industryRisks.name: PHI Leakage in Agent Outputs
industryRisks.description: Pydantic AI agents inadvertently return unredacted patient data due to validation failures on unexpected LLM outputs.
industryRisks.example: Patient SSN exposed in structured response, triggering HIPAA breach notification and $1.5M fine.

industryRisks.name: Unauditable AI Decisions
industryRisks.description: Tool retry logic masks persistent errors, making it impossible to trace AI reasoning for medical board review.
industryRisks.example: Misdiagnosis recommendation not traceable, leading to malpractice liability.

industryRisks.name: Streaming Validation Failures
industryRisks.description: Mid-generation validation breaks structured healthcare responses, causing incomplete patient summaries.
industryRisks.example: Partial treatment plan delivered to physician, delaying critical care.

industryRisks.name: Dependency Context Loss
industryRisks.description: Nested tool calls lose patient context, generating incorrect medication recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Wrong dosage calculated due to missing allergy context from prior tool call.

implementation.title: PHI De-identification Validator
implementation.description: Add Vex result_validator to block PHI before LLM processing, compliant with HIPAA de-identification standards.

implementation.title: Auditable Decision Logging
implementation.description: Capture all agent decisions with Vex for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trails.

implementation.title: Streaming Response Validation
implementation.description: Ensure streaming patient summaries pass validation chunks for SOC 2 availability.
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, StreamingAgent
from vex import Guardrail

class StreamingPatientAgent(StreamingAgent):
    @Guardrail(name="streaming_phi_check")
    def validate_stream_chunk(self, chunk: str) -> bool:
        # Safe for streaming - lightweight PHI check
        return "SSN" not in chunk and "DOB" not in chunk

    result_validators = [ResultValidator(guardrail="streaming_phi_check")]

    async def stream_patient_summary(self, mrn: str):
        chunks = [
            "Patient presents with",
            "stable vital signs:",
            "BP 120/80, HR 72"
        ]
        for chunk in chunks:
            yield chunk
            # Vex validates each chunk automatically

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Sarah Chen's hospital deployed Pydantic AI agents to summarize 10,000 daily patient charts for triage prioritization. The agents processed raw EHR data containing PHI, generating structured summaries for physicians. However, the LLM occasionally hallucinated patient names and SSNs in outputs, and tool retry logic masked validation failures during peak hours. Nested dosage calculations lost allergy context from prior tool calls, creating dangerous recommendations. Without audit trails, the hospital couldn't prove compliance during a surprise HIPAA audit, risking $2M in fines.

The streaming summaries frequently failed mid-generation when validation rejected unexpected formats, leaving physicians with incomplete patient data during emergencies. PHI flowed unprotected to the LLM provider, violating data residency requirements.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex result_validators, PHI detection blocked 99.7% of leaks before LLM processing. All decisions became fully auditable with immutable logs satisfying FDA 21 CFR Part 11. Streaming validation passed on first attempt 98% of the time, ensuring complete summaries reached physicians. The hospital passed its HIPAA audit with zero findings and reduced malpractice exposure by 85%. Vex alerts enabled proactive fixes for the three remaining edge cases, preventing patient harm.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and add result_validators to your Pydantic AI agents to eliminate compliance risks.
